text:
We present CodeT5+, a new family of encoder-decoder open large language models (LLMs) designed to address the limitations of current code models that are typically trained for either the encoding or the decoding tasks only. Our model is trained in a flexible way, that can be used in the form of an encoder only, a decoder only or an encoder-decoder, which is not common for most current models. CodeT5+ is trained with a rich mixture of pretraining objectives, i.e., span denoising, causal language modeling, contrastive learning and text-code matching on unimodal code and bimodal text-code datasets.

Our work seeks to scale large language models for code intelligence and proposes a compute-efficient training methodology that leverages off-the-shelf frozen pre-trained large language models (LLMs) and also focuses on instruction tuning for natural language alignment. Using over 20 code intelligence benchmarks, we demonstrate that CodeT5+ outperforms the state of the art in a diverse set of settings including zero-shot and retrieval-augmented generation. In particular, our instruction-tuned 16B model becomes the new open-source performance benchmark for the HumanEval task, achieving 35.0% pass@1 and surpassing the state-of-the-art from OpenAI’s code-cushman-001. Our models and code are open for the community to utilize.
